message:
feat(cleanup): cleanup_apply_with_cx Cx-first variant (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Complete the cleanup.rs Cx-first pair started in tick 49. Parallel
of `cleanup_preview_with_cx` for the delete path.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `cleanup_apply_with_cx(cx, storage, config)` — mirrors the
    legacy `cleanup_apply` but gates each table's count+prune pair
    behind `cx.checkpoint()?`. Adds an initial checkpoint at the
    top (pre-cancelled cx short-circuits before any DELETE runs)
    and per-section checkpoints before `output_segments`,
    `audit_actions`, `usage_metrics`, and `notification_history`.

  * The `record_maintenance` trailer deliberately does NOT gate on
    a checkpoint — maintenance is best-effort, so a late-cancelled
    caller still gets a maintenance row recording what was actually
    deleted before cancellation propagates up through the return.

Key cancellation-safety property: because each checkpoint fires
BEFORE the next table's count+prune pair starts, a mid-way cancel
leaves partial state that is always *per-table coherent* — a table
either has both its count + prune recorded in the returned
CleanupPlan, or neither. No table is left in the weird state of
"counted but not pruned" or vice-versa (both calls for a given
table are on the same side of any checkpoint).

Regression test (new)
`cleanup_apply_with_cx_matches_legacy_on_empty_db`:

  * Sets up an empty StorageHandle.
  * Runs BOTH `cleanup_apply(&storage, &config)` and
    `cleanup_apply_with_cx(&cx, &storage, &config)` with a fresh
    `Cx::for_request()`.
  * Because apply is idempotent on an empty DB (0 rows eligible, 0
    deleted), the two plans must match bit-for-bit across every
    field and every table summary.
  * Asserts `dry_run: false` explicitly (the field equality alone
    would match if both were true; this is the one apply-specific
    invariant the preview test doesn't need to check).

With tick 50, the `cleanup.rs` public async surface (preview +
apply) has full Cx-first siblings. Follow-on when StorageHandle
gains _with_cx variants: thread cx *into* each count/prune call so
cancellation reaches the SQLite driver, not just between queries.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * cleanup:: — 96 passed / 0 failed (tick 49: 95 -> tick 50: 96,
    +1 new apply-parity test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
